title: Payment processing outage during Q4 settlement
summary: A failed Spring Boot actuator endpoint caused cascading timeouts across the payment pipeline. Root cause: unpatched CVE in Spring Boot 3.1.x. 47-minute outage affecting $2.3M in transactions.

title: jPOS upgrade deferred from Q1 maintenance
summary: Planned upgrade of jPOS from 2.1.7 to 2.1.9 was deferred due to incomplete regression testing on ISO-8583 message formatting. Re-scheduled to Q2 window.

title: Auth Service Upgrade Procedure
summary: Pre-requisites: coordinate with IAM team for token rotation. Must perform rolling restart across 3 AZs. Session cache must be warmed before cutting traffic. Estimated duration: 90 minutes with 2-person team.

title: XSS vulnerability discovered in customer dashboard
summary: Penetration test revealed stored XSS via Express request parsing. Temporary WAF rule deployed. Permanent fix requires Express upgrade to 4.21.1+.

title: TensorFlow model retraining pipeline updated
summary: Model serving infrastructure upgraded to support TF 2.17. Next planned upgrade to 2.18 blocked by model serialization format change. Requires ML team coordination for model re-export.

title: Keycloak session fixation near-miss
summary: Security audit identified session fixation edge case in Keycloak 26.0.x. No exploitation detected but SOC2 auditor flagged as finding. Remediation deadline: April 30.

title: Core Banking Maintenance Procedure
summary: Requires 4-hour window minimum. Must coordinate with batch processing schedule — no changes during month-end (25th-3rd). COBOL runtime changes require mainframe team on-call. Last successful change window: November 2025.

title: Redshift JDBC driver update scheduled
summary: Driver update from 2.1.0.28 to 2.1.0.30 completed successfully. Next planned update to 2.1.0.33 for TLS certificate validation fix pending security review.

title: SendGrid SDK Upgrade Guide
summary: Low-risk upgrade. No breaking API changes between 8.0 and 8.2. Can be deployed during normal business hours with blue-green deployment. Rollback: revert container image tag.

title: Airflow DAG execution privilege escalation
summary: Internal security scan detected that Airflow 2.9.x allows authenticated users to execute arbitrary Python in DAG definitions. Mitigated by restricting DAG upload permissions. Permanent fix: upgrade to 2.10.1.
